items.title: #NSTviral: Remember petition writers? This 87-year-old is keeping the tradition alive [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An 87-year-old "uncle" has warmed hearts online after a video of him patiently helping members of the public fill out forms and write letters at a government office near Jalan Duta went viral. The service, commonly known as petition writing, was once a familiar sight near government offices, where members of the public sought help to complete official forms, letters and documents. Thank you, uncle.

items.title: Sarawak Oil Palms, Hibiscus poised to extend rally, says RHB Research
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sarawak Oil Palms Bhd and Hibiscus Petroleum Bhd could extend their recent rallies after breaking above key technical resistance levels, with RHB Research expecting further upside for both counters if buying momentum is sustained. Sarawak Oil Palms emerged as one of the research firm's top technical picks after the plantation stock broke above the RM4.85 resistance level on strong trading volume, signalling continued bullish sentiment. RHB Research analyst Aiman Kamil Ahmad Shauqi said the stock formed a higher-high white candlestick and closed above the resistance level, reinforcing the positive technical setup.

items.title: NGO urges whole-school safeguarding approach after school stabbing in Banting
items.summary: “To strengthen the child protection ecosystem in schools, BJCK proposes that every school have a comprehensive Child Protection Policy that is consistently implemented, with clear procedures for prevention, reporting, risk management, and intervention,” it said in a statement yesterday. BJCK said other measures should include appointing a designated officer or team responsible for protection and safety in every school to serve as a focal point for coordinating child protection issues, managing complaints and overseeing follow-up action. It also said school management should strengthen regular training for administrators, teachers, and support staff on child protection, early detection of risk factors, trauma-informed practice, and conflict management.

items.title: RM114mil from 'Sabah Watergate' seizure still with MACC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The RM114 million in cash and assets seized in 2016 as part of the probe into a former Sabah Water Department director remains in the custody of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said seized assets were normally held by enforcement agencies until the court process was concluded. "However, the court ordered the forfeiture application to be stayed pending the completion of all appeal processes in the higher courts," Azalina said.

items.title: ICE agent fatally shoots Mexican motorist in Texas during vehicle stop
items.summary: HOUSTON: A US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agent fatally shot a motorist in Houston on Tuesday while officers were trying to stop the man's vehicle, the agency said, amid an escalating federal crackdown on migrants. The man killed was identified as Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, whom ICE described as a Mexican national and "illegal alien" who attempted to evade arrest during a "targeted enforcement operation" by federal immigration officers. According to ICE's account of the incident, Salgado "rammed an ICE law enforcement vehicle, refused to follow multiple verbal commands, and weaponized his vehicle in an attempt to run over an ICE law enforcement officer".

items.title: Indonesian couple, Chinese man held over alleged pickpocketing at Melaka’s Jonker Walk
items.summary: MELAKA, July 8 — Police arrested three foreign nationals, comprising an Indonesian married couple and a Chinese citizen, for alleged involvement in pickpocketing activities at the tourist area of Jalan Hang Jebat, or Jonker Walk, on July 4. Acting Melaka Tengah police chief Supt Halim Abas said the Indonesian couple, both aged 37, and a 27-year-old Chinese man, were arrested at about 3 pm the same day following cooperation between the public and police. He said preliminary investigations found that the Indonesian couple is believed to be involved in several pickpocketing cases in Melaka.

items.title: Zahid wants part of FGV-managed land returned to Felda to strengthen finances
items.summary: MARAN, July 8 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has proposed that a portion of the land currently managed by FGV Holdings Berhad (FGV) be returned to the Federal Land Development Authority (Felda). Ahmad Zahid, who is also Minister of Rural and Regional Development, said the proposal is among measures aimed at strengthening Felda’s financial position. Meanwhile, Ahmad Zahid also said the government is assisting Koperasi Permodalan Felda (KPF) in restructuring several assets to enable the cooperative to repay its members’ share redemptions.

items.title: Govt to ensure sufficient petrol levels until December
items.summary: It said the country's oil and fuel supplies remained stable and sufficient to meet domestic demand as of June 2026, following the government's proactive measures implemented in phases since disruptions began in the Strait of Hormuz on Feb 28, 2026. Supply security is being ensured via a combination of three key approaches: diversifying import sources, optimising domestic fuel production through biodiesel, and implementing long-term supply agreements. "The government has implemented several measures to safeguard oil supply security, including diversifying petroleum supply sources, optimising domestic fuel supply through biodiesel, preventing leakages through enforcement, and carrying out data-driven monitoring," it said.

items.title: Paloh Pakatan candidate Ruban hospitalised after slipped disc flares up during campaign
items.summary: KLUANG, July 8 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Paloh state assembly seat, Dr. A. Ruban, was admitted to a hospital in Batu Pahat at noon yesterday due to health complications arising from a slipped disc. His campaign manager, Abdul Majid Abd Aziz, confirmed the matter when contacted by Bernama and informed that Dr. Ruban had to receive treatment after experiencing pain that had hindered his movement since yesterday morning. Abdul Majid said Dr. Ruban has a history of spinal issues and had undergone surgery previously, but the pain recurred, believed to be due to the strenuous campaign schedule for the state polls.

items.title: Ringgit opens higher against major currencies, easier versus US dollar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened higher against major currencies but eased against the US dollar on Wednesday as demand for the greenback strengthened amid escalating geopolitical tensions in West Asia. At 8am, the local currency weakened to 4.0745/0830 against the US dollar from Tuesday's close of 4.0685/0735. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said global financial markets were expected to remain in risk-off mode following US military strikes against Iranian forces and the US Treasury Department's decision to revoke a waiver on Iranian oil sales.

items.title: Pakatan candidate Maszlee hopes Johor election dialogue sparks more mature political culture
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 8 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Puteri Wangsa state seat, Maszlee Malik, hopes the political dialogue session he participated in yesterday will serve as a catalyst for fostering a more mature political culture in the country. “We want to cultivate a culture of dialogue and political debate to help mature the electorate and encourage voters to make their choices based on arguments, facts and evidence, instead of mere sentiment and emotion,” he told reporters after the Johor State Election Dialogue here yesterday. For the latest news on the 16th Johor state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor/.

items.title: Azalina: RCI decision depends on probe findings, public interest
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Any decision to establish a Royal Commission of Inquiry (RCI) into allegations of a corporate mafia within the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) will be considered based on legal procedures, ongoing investigations and the findings of the relevant authorities. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the government remained open to establishing an RCI or any other appropriate mechanism if deemed necessary in the public interest. She said the government took seriously allegations involving the corporate mafia issue and matters that could undermine public confidence in government institutions.

items.title: MyDigital ID expands to 19 state-level platforms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MyDigital ID is being expanded to state-level platforms, enabling access to online services provided by local authorities, Land and Mines Offices and state agencies. "The government will ensure the MyDigital ID system remains available at an optimal level to enable various government online service systems to be accessed seamlessly," he said in a written parliamentary reply today. He said the government had taken several strategic measures to ensure government online platforms could be accessed using MyDigital ID as a digital identity authentication mechanism.

items.title: Puteri Wangsa Pakatan candidate Maszlee says Johor manifesto built on realistic, deliverable promises
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 8 — The Pakatan Harapan (PH) manifesto for the Johor state election has been drafted based on the needs of the people and includes a monitoring mechanism to ensure that each offer can be implemented. PH candidate for Puteri Wangsa state seat Maszlee Malik said the manifesto does not just contain election promises, but rather offers realistic commitments that can be implemented with the cooperation of the federal government and relevant agencies. Maszlee, who is also the former Education Minister, said among the main offers in the manifesto were the state health scheme, first home assistance for Johor residents, youth development funds, affordable homes and improvements to the education system that are adapted to current needs.

items.title: Govt says Malaysia’s fuel supply secure through December 2026
items.summary: It said the country’s oil and fuel supplies remained stable and sufficient to meet domestic demand as of June 2026, following the government’s proactive measures implemented in phases since disruptions began in the Strait of Hormuz on Feb 28, 2026. Supply security is being ensured via a combination of three key approaches: diversifying import sources, optimising domestic fuel production through biodiesel, and implementing long-term supply agreements. “The government has implemented several measures to safeguard oil supply security, including diversifying petroleum supply sources, optimising domestic fuel supply through biodiesel, preventing leakages through enforcement, and carrying out data-driven monitoring,” it said.

items.title: Body of Iran's Khamenei arrives in Iraq ahead of funeral procession
items.summary: NAJAF: The body of Iran's supreme leader Ali Khamenei arrived at Najaf International Airport in Iraq on Tuesday night, Iraqi state media reported, ahead of funeral processions in the country's holy cities that are home to shrines revered by Shia Muslims. Iran began six days of public funeral ceremonies for Khamenei on Saturday, including a dedicated day in neighbouring Iraq, a Shia powerhouse with close ties to Tehran. Iraqi state TV broadcast the arrival of the Mahan Air flight that brought Khamenei's coffin to Najaf airport, where Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian had arrived shortly before to attend the official Iraqi ceremony.

items.title: Gold slips as Middle East tensions lift oil prices, Fed minutes awaited
items.summary: NEW YORK: Gold fell on Tuesday as investors monitored escalating hostilities in the Middle East that sent oil prices higher, while awaiting minutes from the US Federal Reserve's June meeting for guidance on the monetary policy outlook. Spot gold was down 0.5 per cent at US$4,144.36 per ounce by 2pm ET (1800 GMT). US gold futures for August delivery settled 0.3 per cent lower at US$4,157.40 an ounce.

items.title: Oil jumps and bonds dip as US strikes Iran
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Oil prices jumped and bond futures slid on Wednesday after the US struck Iran and reinstated trade sanctions following attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, while stocks wobbled as momentum ebbed from the record-breaking AI rally. US crude futures were up 2.7 per cent at US$72.40 a barrel and 10-year Treasury futures slid seven ticks as traders priced in the risk of higher inflation and interest rates. Data this week showed stocks of crude in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve had fallen to their lowest level since 1983.

items.title: Dollar at week-high after US resumes attacks on Iran
items.summary: Against the yen, the US dollar strengthened 0.1 per cent to 162.28 yen. The euro slipped 0.1 per cent to US$1.1405, while the British pound edged 0.1 per cent lower to US$1.3353. Bitcoin was down 0.2 per cent at US$63,518.35, while ether was 0.5 per cent lower at US$1,774.45.

items.title: LPT3 implementation, vape issue among focus of Dewan Rakyat today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The proposed implementation of the East Coast Expressway Phase 3 (LPT3) through a public-private partnership (PPP) model and measures to curb syndicates distributing harmful vape products targeting youths and school students are among the focus of the Dewan Rakyat sitting today. According to the Order Paper on the Parliament's official website, during Question Time, Wan Hassan Mohd Ramli (PN-Dungun) will ask the Works Minister to state the government's key considerations for the proposed implementation of LPT3 via the PPP model, including its implications for toll rates and the project's implementation timeline. Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin (PN-Masjid Tanah) will ask the Home Minister to explain the stricter enforcement measures planned to curb syndicates distributing harmful vape products targeting youths and school students.

items.title: Nasdaq sinks as AI worries hit chipmakers
items.summary: Chip stocks in Asia and the US dropped after memory chip giant Samsung Electronics' blowout earnings report failed to satisfy investors with sky-high expectations. Micron dropped 4.7 per cent and Sandisk lost 7.3 per cent. That helped send the PHLX chip index down 4.65 per cent, reducing its 2026 gain to about 74 per cent.

items.title: France to return Syria €50mil stolen by Assad's uncle
items.summary: DAMASCUS: French President Emmanuel Macron said Tuesday during a visit to Syria that Paris will return more than 50 million euros (US$57 million) confiscated from the family of former ruler Bashar al-Assad to the Syrian people. One of them involves the return of money that France confiscated from Rifaat al-Assad, the ousted leader's uncle. Two years later, Rifaat appeared in a family photo alongside Bashar, the ruler's wife Asma and other relatives.

items.title: WCT's RM926mil Abu Dhabi contract to boost earnings over next three years: PublicInvest
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: WCT Holdings Bhd's latest contract win in Abu Dhabi is expected to provide a meaningful boost to its earnings over the next three years, said Public Investment Bank Bhd (Public Invest). The contract, WCT's second major subcontract in Abu Dhabi, involves the construction of six residential buildings for the Yas Riva Residences project, which is being developed by Aldar Development. The project was awarded to the 50:50 unincorporated joint venture Construction General Contracting House Ltd (CGCH)-WCT joint venture.

items.title: One palm, one voice, one vision
items.summary: FOR Malaysia, palm oil is not merely an industry. Yet palm oil does not suffer from having too few stories. That is why palm oil needs one palm, one voice, one vision.

items.title: Bomb attack rocks Damascus during Macron visit
items.summary: DAMASCUS: Two bombs exploded on Tuesday near a hotel in Damascus where French President Emmanuel Macron spent the night, wounding 18 people and overshadowing the first visit to Syria by a European Union head of state since Bashar al-Assad was toppled. His office said he had not heard the blasts. Sharaa said investigations were ongoing.

items.title: Malaysia overhauls auto incentives to deepen local supply chain [WATCH]
items.summary: RAWANG, Selangor: The government is refining its New Customised Incentive Mechanism (NCIM) to ensure investments in the automotive industry deliver stronger domestic industrial capabilities while accelerating the growth of the local supply chain. Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said the enhanced incentive framework forms part of Malaysia's broader strategy to position itself as a gateway to the Asean market of more than 700 million people. The government's long-standing commitment to developing the automotive industry was never solely about vehicle production, Johari said.

items.title: Pakistan-registered cargo plane missing off Karachi coast after navigation problem
items.summary: MEXICO CITY: A Pakistan-registered Boeing 737 cargo plane with five crew members on board lost contact with air traffic control on Tuesday night after reporting a navigational system problem on its way from Sharjah to Karachi, Pakistan aviation authorities said. Authorities have launched a coordinated search and rescue operation at sea through various agencies to locate the missing plane, the Pakistan Airports Authority said on Facebook. The plane reported a navigational system issue at 9.18pm Pakistan Standard Time (1618 GMT) while flying towards Karachi, the airports authority said.

items.title: US launches new strikes on Iran after reinstating oil sanctions over shipping attacks
items.summary: TEHERAN: The US military unleashed a new wave of strikes against Iran on Tuesday and revoked a licence allowing the country to sell oil after three tankers were hit in the Strait of Hormuz, putting pressure on an already fragile ceasefire. "Iran's demonstrated aggression was unwarranted, dangerous, and a clear violation of the ceasefire," Centcom said on X. Iranian media reported explosions early Wednesday local time in the southern port city of Sirik, on Qeshm Island, and in Bandar Abbas. A US official said earlier negotiators continued to work in good faith towards a final agreement with Iran.

items.title: Beijing is looking at curbing overseas access to China's top AI models
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Chinese authorities have held meetings with top tech firms over the past month about potentially restricting overseas access to China's most advanced AI models, including those yet to be released, three people familiar with the discussions said. Since the emergence of DeepSeek's R1 model last year, Chinese AI models have made big inroads globally thanks to their low costs and increasing capabilities. TOUGHENING UP PENALTIES FOR AI THEFT DISCUSSED At the meetings, led by China's Ministry of Commerce, participants discussed putting limits on the most advanced AI models – both closed-source and more open versions, according to two of the sources.

items.title: Johor election: Parties battle for decisive swing seats
items.summary: Campaign efforts have now shifted towards a final push to sway voters in several marginal seats that could determine the scale of victory this Saturday. Of the 44 seats, between 29 and 30 seats are expected to favour Barisan Nasional. Among them are seven seats considered BN's safest constituencies — Machap, Buloh Kasap, Tanjung Surat, Larkin, Benut, Pulai Sebatang and Kukup.

items.title: Ukrainian capital Kyiv under missile attack, two injured
items.summary: KYIV: Ukraine's capital Kyiv came under a Russian missile attack early on Wednesday, triggering fires and injuring at least two people, Mayor Vitali Klitschko said. Two people were injured, with one requiring treatment in hospital. A missile strike in the southern port of Odesa earlier in the evening injured 10 people, regional governor Oleh Kiper said.

items.title: Banting school stabbing: Victim's father denies bullying claims
items.summary: BANTING: The father of the Form 3 sudent who was stabbed in the SMK Bandar Banting incident on Monday denies that his daughter is a bully, as alleged by unverified notes circulating online. Mohd Firuz Ibrahim said his daughter was a quiet student who rarely socialised outside of her family circle. Firuz had earlier said his daughter's name was No.

items.title: Banting school stabbing: Suspect allegedly hunted for specific targets [WATCH]
items.summary: BANTING: Students who witnessed Monday's rampage at SMK Bandar Banting said the knife-wielding suspect appeared to be hunting for certain individuals before stabbing the only victim. A Form Four student said the suspect, who is a former student, entered the school during recess between 9.30am and 10am. Another student, a Form Three boy, said the victim was the suspect's best friend.

items.title: As Malaysia nears zero hardcore poverty, economists say it should be viewed as a milestone and not a destination
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — Buoyed by Malaysia’s strong economic performance, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced last year that his administration would fully end hardcore poverty in the country by the first quarter of 2026. In 2024, two years after assuming office, Anwar announced that Kuala Lumpur, Melaka and Negeri Sembilan had fully eradicated hardcore poverty. Malaysia defines hardcore poverty based on a household’s ability to earn the minimum income needed to meet their basic food requirements – also called the Food Poverty Line Income – which stands at RM1,236 as of 2024.

items.title: 'AI, digital tools can help popularise, preserve calligraphy'
items.summary: ARTIFICIAL intelligence (AI), digital platforms and creative media can be harnessed to preserve and popularise calligraphy among the younger generation. National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang said digitising calligraphy works, organising virtual exhibitions and developing learning applications through technological collaborations could help preserve the traditional art. Aaron said the Unity Calligraphy Festival 2026 celebrated the Jawi, Chinese, Tamil and other traditional scripts as they reflected the cultural identity of Malaysia's diverse communities.

items.title: Cybersecurity law review may introduce whipping, victim compensation
items.summary: The government is reviewing cybersecurity laws, including the possibility of introducing whipping for offenders and victim compensation. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the Legal Affairs Division was conducting an in-depth study on providing comprehensive protection for cybercrime victims. Azalina added that the current domestic legal framework focused on punitive action against perpetrators under the Penal Code and Criminal Procedure Code, while victim protection still required closer scrutiny.

items.title: S. Korea trade mission secures RM58m in sales
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A market immersion programme in South Korea, jointly organised by the Malaysia External Trade Development Corp (Matrade) and Mara, generated more than RM58 million in sales for participating Malaysian companies. The partnership aims to strengthen Malaysian companies' export readiness and support overseas expansion. Five Malaysian companies took part in the mission.

items.title: George Town celebrates 18 years as a Unesco world heritage site
items.summary: Eighteen years after Unesco recognition, George Town has grown into a thriving living heritage city balancing preservation, culture, tourism, and economic development. Adaptive reuse of heritage buildings and government incentives have boosted conservation, creative industries, investment, and community-focused urban renewal. GEORGE TOWN, July 7 — George Town has maintained its status as a Unesco World Heritage Site for 18 years, but the historic city has achieved far more than preserving its heritage buildings.

items.title: ‘If it’s from the PM, I will act on it’: Ex-finance minister Tengku Zafrul says in Muhyiddin’s trial
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz today confirmed he did have the power when he was finance minister to not act on companies’ applications for government projects, but said he would ensure that the Finance Ministry considers the applications if they were forwarded by the prime minister. Tengku Zafrul, 53, said this while testifying as the prosecution’s 11th witness in Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia president and former prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s power abuse and money laundering trial. Tengku Zafrul was previously the finance minister from March 2020 to August 2021 during Muhyiddin’s administration.

items.title: Student hurt after mercury spill in Terengganu school chemistry lab
items.summary: DUNGUN, July 7 — A female student was injured after coming into contact with mercury spilled on a laboratory table at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Bukit Besi here today. Al Muktafi Billah Shah Fire and Rescue Station Operations Commander Kama Suhaimi Abdullah said the Form Four student was sent to a nearby clinic for treatment. He said firefighters carried out cleaning works at the spill site using prescribed hazardous material handling procedures.

items.title: Anwar says Putrajaya mulling Land Act amendment for multiple houses on Felda lots
items.summary: Meanwhile, Anwar said the Felda New Generation Housing Project (PGBF), introduced in 2013, covers 43 sites involving a total of 8,224 housing units across seven states, namely Pahang, Johor, Negeri Sembilan, Kedah, Terengganu, Kelantan and Perak. Anwar said for this year, PGBF would be implemented in Besout, Perak, involving 102 units, followed by Sungai Koyan, Pahang (68 units), and Pasoh, Negeri Sembilan (540 units). “Once these are completed, construction will continue at Sungai Koyan (89 units), Maokil, Johor (540 units), Bukit Jalor, Negeri Sembilan (239 units), Sungai Tekam, Pahang (100 units), and Papan Timur, Johor (539 units).

items.title: Malaysia schools need proactive safety policy, not reactive responses, says official
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The government has been urged to draw up a National School Safety Master Plan as a comprehensive policy to strengthen the protection of students following several incidents of violence in schools that have sparked public concern in recent times. Federal Territories State Leadership Council Education Bureau chairman Zaleha Dullah said the master plan should encompass physical security, risk management, emergency procedures and a standardised monitoring mechanism for implementation across all schools. She said the plan could be formulated through a National School Safety Roundtable involving the Ministry of Education (MOE), security agencies, psychologists, academics, parents’ associations, civil society organisations and student representatives.

items.title: Johor election: KTMB ramps up ETS services with 20pc discount
items.summary: Following a total sell-out of an earlier batch of tickets on June 19, KTMB is deploying eight additional services across two key routes: KL Sentral–JB Sentral–KL Sentral and JB Sentral–Gemas–JB Sentral. For those planning their trip, ticket sales for the JB Sentral–Gemas–JB Sentral route opened today at 3:00 pm. Tickets for the KL Sentral–JB Sentral–KL Sentral route will go on sale Wednesday at 9:00 am.

items.title: Corporate espionage case: Ex-Petronas manager sent upstream performance report to Petros email, witness says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The Sessions Court was told today that Mohd Khairul Akmal Mohd Jasni, a former Petronas manager, had applied to work at Petroleum Sarawak Berhad (Petros) months before he allegedly leaked the national oil company’s financial and business secrets to the same company. Petros Human Resource Analyst Nurul Qurratu Aini Laila Junaidi, 40, testified that Mohd Khairul Akmal had submitted an application for the Head Upstream Finance position through its website’s internal system on Jan 24, 2024. “On March 27, 2024, I contacted Mohd Khairul Akmal via email and telephone to extend a job offer for the position of Head Finance (Gas Aggregator & Sarawak Resource Management (SRM)), with a monthly salary of RM25,000.

items.title: ‘Preparations made’: Nadma says govt agencies ready for possible ‘Super El Nino’
items.summary: PUCHONG, July 7 — Government agencies responsible for disaster management are on high alert ahead of the possible onset of a ‘Super El Nino’ towards the end of the year, while also stepping up preparations to mitigate the risks of extreme heat, haze and open burning during the Southwest Monsoon. National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma) director-general Meor Ismail Meor Akim said the coordination meeting held today was not because agencies were unprepared, but to update the public on their current state of readiness and strengthen coordination among the ministries and agencies involved. “All agencies have taken the necessary measures.

items.title: Sharing Banting stabbing children’s identities could land you in jail, warns Suhakam
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Malaysia’s human rights commission has warned that sharing photographs, videos or other identifying information about the children involved in the recent Banting school stabbing could be a criminal offence. In a statement today, the Office of the Children’s Commissioner of the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) urged the public to stop circulating content that could harm both the victim and the alleged attacker. It said both children were entitled to legal protections under the Child Act 2001 and the UN Convention on the Rights of the Child, regardless of the ongoing police investigation.

items.title: Zahid pays tribute to former journalist and Umno media officer Jailani Harun
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has expressed his condolences to the family of former journalist and media officer at the Umno President’s Office Datuk Jailani Harun, who died today. “I extend my heartfelt condolences to the entire family of the late Jailani. Jailani died at 11.34am at Serdang Hospital.

items.title: Incoming: Super El Niño could bring heat and haze from November, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Malaysia could face hotter weather, lower rainfall and a greater risk of transboundary haze from late this year as MetMalaysia forecasts a Super El Niño to develop in November and last until January 2027. Ambun said El Niño conditions were already present, but Super El Niño status was only expected to begin around November. If realised, the phenomenon could bring significantly lower rainfall and higher temperatures to Malaysia early next year.

items.title: Teacher, accomplice jailed eight years for trafficking woman for sexual exploitation
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, July 7 — The Sessions Court here today found a teacher and his accomplice guilty of trafficking a woman for sexual exploitation and sentenced them to eight years’ imprisonment and RM5,000 fine each. Judge Mohd Zul Zakiqudin Zulkifli handed down the sentence on Mohd Hasfyanizam Mansor, 50, and labourer Mohamad Fazley Jaafar, 31, at the end of the defence case. Deputy public prosecutors Ahmad Hafizudin Manjur Ahmad and Noradila Abdul Latif prosecuted, while lawyer Mohd Shahir Mat Jusoh represented the two accused.

items.title: Three men plead not guilty to setting fire to activist Cikgu Chandra’s wife’s cars
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Three men pleaded not guilty today in the Petaling Jaya Sessions Court to charges of setting fire to three cars belonging to the wife of activist ‘Cikgu Chandra’ in March. The accused, tow-truck driver A. Kumaran, 34; security guard S. Kisshen Nair, 32; and lorry driver P. Surendran, 37, are jointly charged with committing mischief by fire on two Perodua vehicles and a Proton automobile owned by M. Sri Uma Mahesvary, 50. Judge Noor Ruzilawati Mohd Nor allowed RM8,000 in bail for each accused, subject to the additional conditions sought by the prosecution.

items.title: Thunderstorms and heavy rain forecast in nine states, KL and Putrajaya until 6pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds have been forecasted in nine states, Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya till 6pm. The Malaysian Meteorological Department issued the warning for Johor, Negeri Sembilan, Perak (Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim), Kelantan (Tumpat, Pasir Mas, Jeli, Tanah Merah, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang), Terengganu (Setiu, Kuala Nerus, Kuala Terengganu, Marang, Dungun and Kemaman), Pahang (Tanah Tinggi Cameron, Bentong, Temerloh, Kuantan, Bera, Pekan and Rompin), and Selangor (Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling, Hulu Langat and Sepang). Over in Sarawak, the warning includes Kuching, Sarikei (Julau), Sibu (Kanowit), Mukah (Mukah), Kapit (Song), Bintulu (Bintulu dan Sebauh), Miri (Subis, Beluru, Miri and Marudi) and Limbang.

items.title: MACC, Armed Forces tighten information-sharing in fight against corruption
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 7 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) and the Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) have reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening strategic cooperation, particularly in intelligence sharing, information exchange and governance enhancement, to bolster anti-corruption efforts and safeguard national interests. MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said the commission highly values its longstanding cooperation with the Armed Forces and expressed hope that the synergy between the two agencies will continue to grow stronger. Also present were MACC Intelligence Division senior director Datuk Saiful Ezral Arifin and MAF Security and Counter Intelligence Directorate director Lt Col Muhamad Zainol Md Yusof.

items.title: EC says Johor’s 56-seat election could be decided by 10pm on Saturday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July7 — Voters are expected to know the outcome of all 56 seats in Saturday’s Johor state election by about 10pm, after the Election Commission of Malaysia upgraded its election results management system. According to Buletin TV3 , Election Commission chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the improvements should enable the commission to announce the full outcome by around 10pm. Buletin TV3 said Ramlan also urged eligible voters to turn out in large numbers on Saturday to fulfil their democratic responsibility.

items.title: New mosques, surau must include facilities for PWDs and seniors, Dewan Rakyat told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — All new mosque and surau developments in the country must incorporate facilities and infrastructure that cater to persons with disabilities (PWDs) and senior citizens to ensure places of worship are more inclusive and accessible, the Dewan Rakyat was told today. She said that, to date, the majority of mosques and surau registered under the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Department (JAWI) have already provided basic facilities for senior citizens and PWDs. However, she said the government is now prioritising efforts to upgrade these facilities through existing funding mechanisms, including maintenance allocations, mosque funds, community contributions and financial assistance coordinated by the respective state religious authorities within their jurisdictions.

items.title: EC aims for 96pc turnout in early voting
items.summary: KLUANG, July 7 — The Election Commission (EC) is targeting a 96 per cent turnout for early voting in the Johor state election today. “Based on our previous experience, early voting turnout can reach as high as 96 per cent, and we are maintaining that projection,” he told a press conference after observing the early voting process at Kem Mahkota here. Commenting on today’s early voting process, Ramlan said 62 early voting centres were opened across the state at 8am.

items.title: In Larkin, land leases and a city’s growing pains shape Johor election contest
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 7 — Land lease renewals in Kampung Melayu Majidee and the need to modernise public infrastructure have emerged as the key issues shaping the contest for the Larkin state seat in the 16th Johor state election. Mohd Hairi, who is also State Youth, Sports, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Committee chairman, said the state government remained committed to safeguarding Kampung Melayu Majidee through lease renewal offers of between 60 and 99 years, either on an individual or lot basis, as well as a 50 per cent premium discount to ease the financial burden on residents. Suhaizan, who is Pulai MP, however, said the state government’s measures had yet to meet residents’ expectations.

items.title: MACC: Three arrested in Kedah over alleged RM20m financing bid for paddy and rice purchases that never happened
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Three people, including two company directors, have been arrested by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) in Kedah over allegations they used false documents in an attempt to obtain about RM20 million in financing for paddy and rice purchases that investigators say never took place. In a statement today, the anti-graft agency said the suspects, who are involved in the rice and paddy production and processing business, are suspected of submitting documents containing false particulars to a development financial institution under the Trade Working Capital Financing-I facility. The financing, worth about RM20 million, was intended for the purchase of paddy and rice.

items.title: ‘The tears just won’t stop’: Father says Banting stabbing victim leaves ICU but remains traumatised
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The 15-year-old girl injured in a stabbing at a secondary school in Banting remains deeply traumatised despite her condition stabilising after surgery, her father said today. The tears just won’t stop flowing,” he said. Mohd Firuz said doctors and nurses were continuing to monitor the teenager’s condition.

items.title: Perak Ruler officiates Sultan Nazrin Shah 69 Commando Camp in Ulu Kinta
items.summary: IPOH, July 7 — The Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah, today officially opened the Sultan Nazrin Shah 69 Commando Camp in Ulu Kinta here. The Raja Permaisuri of Perak, Tuanku Zara Salim, the Raja Muda of Perak, Raja Jaafar Raja Muda Musa, and the Raja DiHilir of Perak, Raja Iskandar Dzurkarnain Sultan Idris Shah also attended the event. In his speech, Saifuddin Nasution said the opening of the camp symbolises the MADANI Government’s commitment to strengthening the capabilities of the nation’s security forces to address increasingly complex and evolving security challenges.
